A virgin's fain to live on bread, but married on (her parents') liver fed. ☞ Anything would do for the unmarried daughter living with her parents, but, as a married daughter, presents must be made to her at every one of numerous Hindū festivals. boṭī utār'nā , Mah. v. a. To bite off a piece of flesh. Kutte ne boṭi utār lī. boṭī boṭī phaṛak'nā , v. a. Mah. To throb in every vein. Jis tan kī javānī meṅ paṛī phaṛke the boṭī, Us tan ko na poshāk, na us peṭ ko roṭī, Bāl uṛ gaë, aur māṅg hai kuchh nochī khasoṭī, Kājal hai, na missī hai, na kaṅghī hai, na choṭī . On old age. Nazīr. The body which in every pulse did rampant throb youthful, In age no garment owns, no bread its stomach's need supplies, Her hair fallen off, the middle part all plucked or straggling lies, Nor lamp-black, nor missī , nor comb, no…

s.m. See s.v. A. bū. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

more: Garh. n. m. S. बिवाह from वह to marry. Marriage; wedlock; matrimonial alliance. 1. Āj kī maṅgnī, kal kā byāh, Ṭūṭ gaī taṅgṛī, rah gayā byāh . Boys' rhyme. To-day betrothed, to-morrow is the wedding day, You break your leg—your marriage you must now delay. 2. Biyāh nahīṅ kiyā to kyā, barāteṅ to dekhī haiṅ . Prov. If I never married, what then? Have n't I seen many a wedding? (If it has n't happened to me, have I not seen it happen to others?) byāh rachānā , v. a. To celebrate a marriage. Lālā jī ne biyāh rachāyā laiṅhgā bech lugāī kā, Māshā bhar kī chār kachaurī, khurmā māshe ḍhāī kā, Ghar meṅ roveṅ bahan, bhānjī, bāhar rove nāī kā , Play. Chaubolā . Did ever you see such a wedding queer, As the Lalā's who sold his wife's skirt for a stiver? A half drachm just of dāl cakes four, Of sweetmeats two and a half, no more, Sisters and nieces crying at home, The barber without with wailing o'erc…
s. m. Marriage. بياه رچانا byāh rachānā, To celebrate a marriage. بياه کرنا byāh karnā, To marry. بياه لانا byāh-lānā, To take in marriage, to bring home a wife, to marry.

more: n. m. S. व्याहृ to utter. See برنن 1. News; tidings; intelligence. 2. A detailed account; a day book. byo'rā, karnā, yā denā , v. a. To inform; intimate. byore-wār , adj. Circumstantial; detailed ( bil tafsīl ); distinct; explicit. byore-wār , adv. In detail; explicitly; distinctly (بالتفصيل).
s. m. 1. Difference, distinction. 2. Account, explanation, history, detail of circumstances.
subs: byorā karnā (-kā), To relate, describe; to inform, intimate
beʼore-wār, adj. & adv. Circumstantial, detailed, explicit, distinct, clear, plain; — circumstantially, in detail, explicitly, &c. (syn. tafṣīl-wār; biʼt-tafṣīl).

def: s.m. = byoṅt, q.v. s.m. The act of cutting out clothes; out, fit, shape, fashion; plan, scheme, contrivance; suitable or proper time or season, opportunity; agreement, concurrence, union; economy, frugality.
v.t. = byoṅtnā, q.v. v.t. To cut out or shape clothes; to measure for, or to fit, clothes; — to cut up, hack, kill, slay.
v.t. = byoṅtnā, q.v. v.t. To cut out or shape clothes; to measure for, or to fit, clothes; — to cut up, hack, kill, slay.

v.t. To have or get clothes cut out, sewn, or made up.

conj. & adv. And, both, also, moreover, as well as (usually placed, like the Latin que and the Greek τε, as an enclitic after the word or clause which it connects with what precedes); otherwise; either, or; even indeed, certainly; just; mutually, equally; but, on the contrary, yet, nevertheless; for, on account of; — adj. & s.m. Seedless; bad, vile, base, mischievous; — a thief; a tortoise; an epithet of Śiva: — ća may also be used as an expletive.
s.m. = ćāʼo, or ćāh, qq.v.
more: s. f. Tea. چادان chā-dān, s. m. Teapot.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چَا (čā), ultimately from Chinese 茶 (chá).

r: ća, bāg̠-ća, īća, bāg̠-īća, za, īza, ja, zha, mashkīza
def: (old P. ća; Zend ća; S. क), A particle affixed to Persian nouns to form diminutives (e.g. bāg̠-ća, 'a small garden': it may also take the form -īća, as bāg̠-īća: it sometimes occurs also in the forms -za, -īza, — the letters ća, ja, za, and zha being interchangeable, — e.g. mashkīza, 'a small leathern bottle').
s.f. Tea
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چَا (čā), ultimately from Chinese 茶 (chá).

ety: rt. of ćabā-nā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्व अइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Chewing, mastication; champing, crunching.
s.f. Throbbing, throb, shoot of pain ( = ṭabhak; ṭapak, qq.v.). s.f. Sound of (water, &c.) dropping; dropping; beat, throb, pulsation, &c. ( = ṭapak, q.v.).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit चिबुक (cibuka).

def: v.n. To shoot with pain, to throb ( = ṭapaknā).

ety: ćabā˚ = Prk. चव्वाव(इ), or चव्वावे(इ) = S. चर्व+आपय(ति), rt. चर्व्
def: v.t. To bite (the lip); to gnaw; to champ; to crunch; — to cause to chew ( = ćabwānā)
more: v. a. 1. To chew; masticate. 2. To bite (the lip).
v. a. To chew, gnaw, champ, masticate, bite (the lip). چبا چباکي بات کرني chabā chabāke bāt karnī, To speak with study or preparation; also, without reserve, or haughtily and scornfully.
subs: To speak with study or preparation; to speak without reserve; to mince one's words, to speak haughtily or scornfully or affectedly; to hum and haw, to drawl; to express what one has to say by little and little; to clip one's words, to mouth, to speak indistinctly.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Childish, boyish, puerile ( = ćibillā).

ety: by redupl. of Prk. चव्वडी = S. चर्व+र+ई
def: s.f. Jawing, jabber, chatter.
more: n. f. Jabber; chatter. Kyā chabaṛ chabaṛ bāteṅ kartā hai .

ety: by redupl. of Prk. चव्वडी = S. चर्व+र+ई
def: s.f. Jawing, jabber, chatter.
s.m. Something to be munched or chewed; parched gram or grain (used as food).
more: n. m. S. चर्ब to chew. 1. Something to munch; parched grain. 2. Fac . The dole to Govt. clerks in the hills. chabe'nī , n. f. A lunch consisting of parched grain or sweetmeats, etc., given at midday.
s. m. Parched grain to exercise the teeth with when more substantial food is not at hand.

def: s.f. Parched grain ( = ćabenā); a lunch of parched grain or sweetmeats, &c., given at noon.
more: s. f. Parched grain. See چبينا chabenā.

s.f. & adv. = ćapaṛ-ćapaṛ, q.v. s.f. The sound made in chewing or eating; the sound of lapping; — adv. (Eating or chewing) with a noise; with the noise of lapping.
v.t. To crunch or crush (with the teeth).
s.f. (local) A key (syn. tāl).
wety: See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

def: s.f. (local) A key (syn. tāl).

more: W., v. a. 1. To chew slowly. 2. To mouth one's words; to mumble.

def: s.f. (prov.) corr. of ćaqmāq, q.v. s.f. A flint, or steel, for striking fire
subs: To strike fire with a flint and steel.

ety: Prk. चव्वणअं = S. चर्वणीयं, rt. चर्व्
def: v.t. To chew, masticate; to press with the teeth, to bite; to champ; to gnaw; to crunch ( = ćabānā)
more: v. a. S. चर्वण Pr. chabbam . 1. To chew; masticate. Naī bahār ke hole (fried peas) chāb! Delhī street cry. 2. To gnaw; crunch; devour; eat up. 1. Tujhe kachche ko chāb jāūṅgā . I'll eat you up raw. 2. Dekhaī Sanichchar jab kuchhuo na millai, Ahe chaukhaṭ kivaṛyā chibavai laglaire kī . Mag. Jantāsārī . And when the god found there was nothing left to eat, He chawed and ate up all the doors and frames complete. chabā-chabā-ke bāteṅ k. , v. n. See under bāt. chabnī haḍḍī , n. f. Cartilage; grissel; gristle. hoṅṭ chabānā , v. a. To bite one's lips.
v. a. To chew, to masticate, to gnaw. چاب چاب باتين کرني chāb chāb bāteṅ karnī, To mince oneʼs words, to speak little and haughtily. ¶ (dakh.) See چبهانا.
subs: ćāb-ćāb bāteṅ karnā = ćabā-ćabā-ke bāteṅ karnā

def: v.n. To be chewed or masticated.
more: v. n. S. चर्व To be chewed.

def: adj. Requiring to be chewed
s.m. (dialec.) One addicted to joking, a jester.

def: adj. Crushed, bruised; — s.f. Jesting, joking; jest, joke.
adj. & s.m. Chewing, gnawing; — chewer, &c.

def: adj. Quick, active, alert; — s.m. A horse-whip, a whip
more: n. m. A whip; a horsewhip. chābuk-dhaṛī , n. f. lit . the whip-handle. A shrub; cryptospegia grandiflora. chābuk-savār , n. m. A rough rider; one who breaks in horses; a jockey. chābuk-savārī , n. f. Breaking in horses. chābuk mārnā , v. n. To whip; lash; horsewhip.

1. adj. Active, alert. 2. s. m. A horse-whip. (In this sense, however, it is found in the works of ḵẖusrau only, and it is probably Hindī). چابک بازي chābuk-bāzī, s. f. Whipping, lash. چابک دست chābuk-dast, adj. Nimble, active, alert, quick, adroit, beautiful. چابک پهٿکارنا chābuk phaṭkārnā, To crack a whip. چابک سوار chābuk-sawār, s. m. 1. A horsebreaker, a jockey. 2. A good rider. 3. A horse-broker. چابک سواري chābuk-sawār-ī, s. f. Jockeyship, &c. چابک مارنا chābuk mārnā, To whip, lash, flog.
subs: adj. Nimble, active, alert, expeditious; dexterous, adroit; light-fingered; beautiful
s.m. A horse-breaker; a rough-rider; a good rider, a jockey
s.f. The business of a horse-breaker, &c.; breaking-in horses; jockeyship
wety: From Classical Persian چابک (čābuk).

def: s.f. Activity, alertness, agility, celerity, despatch.
more: (from چابک) s. f. Activity, alertness, despatch, celerity, agility.

ety: ćabū˚ = ćawū = ćaʼū = S. चतुर्; cf. ćaubār

more: s. m. 1. A terrace or mound to sit and converse on. 2. A custom house. 3. A police-office.
s. m. An elevated seat or platform, a bench.

(-ko), To convey to the police station.

ety: ćabū˚ = ćawū = ćaʼū = S. चतुर्; cf. ćaubār
def: s.m. A terrace, a raised and levelled piece of ground (to sit and converse on), a platform; a market-place; a boundary mark; a police station; a tribunal or court of justice; a custom house

A police station ( kotvālī ). chabūtrā baṅdhnā , v. n. ( Slang ). To be big with child; to be near one's time; to be pregnant. chabūtre chaṛhānā , v. a. To convey to the Police station.

def: s.m. Paternal uncle, father's (younger) brother; — a superior; a knowing or astute fellow
more: n. m. 1. Paternal uncle; father's brother. Tīr na kamān, mere chachā khūb laṛe! Prov. Nor bow nor arrow his, my uncle a great warrior is! (Said to a boaster). 2. See abbā , (2.). Achchhā, chachā, samjhūṅgā! chachā banānā , v. a. To chastise ( ṭhīk banānā ). Kisī din mujhe mil gaë to chachā hī banā-kar chhoṛūṅgā. chacherā bhāī, chachā-zād bhāī ; Bhoj. pitiāus . n. m. Cousin (father's younger brother's son). Chachere, mamere, baṛ tale bahutere . Wom. Prov. Paternal and maternal cousins all no end, under the Baṛh tree's ample shade extend. (Said to distant relations claiming relationship).
s. m. Paternal uncle, fatherʼs brother.
s. m. (dakh.) Fatherʼs younger brother ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

s.m. A cousin, a father's younger brother's son.

s. m. 1. The pole round which they play at the festival of the holī. 2. A fair or assemblage of people collected after the holī.

s.f. A sheet; a table-cloth; a covering; a coverlet; a calico mantle or wrapper (of one fold, which reaches from the head to the ankles); — a sheet (of iron, &c.); a cascade; a kind of fire-work (in imitation of a cascade); — shot and shell
more: n. f. Dim. chadaryā . 1. A sheet; tablecloth; bed-sheet; coverlet; mantle. 2. Sheet iron 3. A sheet offered up as a cover for a tomb. 4. A plain white calico wrapper, mantle, or cloak that reaches from the head to the ankles. 5. A waterfall. 6. A kind of firework in imitation of a waterfall. 7. Shot and shell. chādar utārnā , v. a. 1. To take off a woman's veil. 2. To insult or disgrace (a woman). chādar joṛā , n. m. A pair of shawls. chādar chaṛhānā , v. a. To offer up a chādar to be spread over the tomb of a saint. chādar-chhat , n. f. A ceiling cloth. chādar ḍālnā yā uṛhānā , Rus. v. a. To marry a widow. chād'rah , n. m. A small sheet or mantle covering the head and shoulders down to the waist. chaddar-chhupavval , n. m. Boys' game.
s. f. 1. A sheet. 2. A cascade. چادربچهانا chādar bichhānā, To lay the cloth.
s. A large piece of cloth or sheet, of one and a half or two breadths, thrown over the head so as to cover the whole body ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: S. इच्छा, rt. इष्; or rt. of ćāhnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Wish, desire, inclination; volition, will; longing, craving; love, affection, liking, fondness; fancy; choice; — appetite, relish, zest, gusto; — want, need, requirement; requisition, demand, request
more: n. f. S. इच्छा 1. Desire; love; liking; affection. Kitnoṅ meṅ baṛh-ke aisī baṛhī ulfatoṅ kī chāh, Jo jism o jān ek hue unke, wāh wāh! Āshaq muā to mar gayā māshūq khā-makhā, Māshūq mar gayā to voh āshiq bhī kar-ke āh, Us gul-badan kī qabar upar jā-ke mar gayā. Jītā rahā na koī har ek ā-ke mar gayā . Death. Nazīr. In many minds the flame of love burned fierce to that degree, Two seeming bodies but one heart—a wondrous sight to see! Her lover dead, his mistress' corpse with his would mingled be, His mistress dead, her love-lorn suitor, wrung with misery, Upon the fair one's grave falls in a swoon, and falls to die. Living not one is left, each one hath come here but to die. 2. Longing; desire; wish ( ishtiyāq ). Jiskī yahāṅ chāh, uskī vahāṅ bhī chāh . Wom. Prov. The loved on earth are loved of Heaven. (Whom the gods love die young). 3. Want; need; exigency; occasion; urgency; necessity. 4.…
s. f. Desire, wish, love, liking, affection, choice, want, appetite. چاه چت chāh-chit, or چاه وچوز chāh-o-choz, Love, affection, dalliance.
s. m. A pit, a well. چاه زنخ chāh-i-zanaḵẖ, or چاه ذقن chāh-i-ẕaḳan, The dimple of the chin. چاه کن chāh-kan, s. m. A well-digger.
subs: adv. With pleasure; with appetite or relish; eagerly; affectionately, lovingly; of one's own accord, voluntarily
(-kī), To have a desire, &c. (for); to be in request or demand.

A wheel (over a well). 3. (Com. nīmchak ). A series of wooden rings forming the wall of a well. 4. A vessel in which sugar is manufactured, after being transferred from the chāshnī or kaṛāhī. chāk pūjnā , The Hindu marriage ceremony of worshipping the potter's wheel.

1. s. m. A narrow opening intentionally left in clothes, a rent, a slit, a fissure. 2. adj. Rent, slit, torn, lacerated. چاک کرنا chāk karnā, To rend, to slit, to tear, to split, rip. چاک گريبان chāk-girebān, Having the collar rent, afflicted, sad.

def: s.m. One habituated to service; one in want of service; one fit for service, a good servant.
more: (from چاکر) s. m. A good servant, a person fit for (in want of) service, one habituated to service.

def: s.f. = ćākrī, q.v. s.f. A pulley; a mill ( = ćakkī); — a round plate on which bread is rolled; a whirligig; a bandalore or small wheel with a cord fixed to its centre which winds and unwinds itself alternately by the motion of the hands; a circle or an assembly of singers; — a kind of cloth made of silk and cotton.
adj. Whirling round, in a whirl, bewildered, dazed, dazzled, astonished
subs: v.n. To be or become dazed or dazzled, to be in a whirl, to be or become astonished or amazed, &c.

def: v.n. To be in a whirl, to be dazzled, &c. ( = ćakāṭe jānā, q.v.s.v. ćakāṭā).
s.m. = ćag̠atta, q.v.
more: adj. 1. Settled; fixed; conclusive. 2. Wholesale. Phr. There being nothing more to be said.
n. m. S. चक्र a circle. 1. A round slice (of a vegetable). 2. Round red or blue spots on the body. chakattā ḍālnā , v. a. To leave a round mark from a bite (from anger or love).
s. m. 1. A scraping of the skin, a scrape, a scar. 2. A slice, &c. چکتا دينا يا لينا chaktā-denā or -lenā, To scar.

def: s.m. A round slice (as of a vegetable, &c.), a slice; round discoloured spot (on the body), a mark, scar, scratch or scrape (of the skin); a sod or turf
subs: (meṅ), To bite so as to leave a mark; to mark, to scar
(or ćaktā) denā (-ko), To scar, to mark

def: s.m.(f.?) = H چکونڐ चकवंड ćakwaṇḍ, चकौंड ćakauṅḍ, s.m. = H چکونڙ चकौंड़ ćakauṅṛ, चकोंड़ ćakoṅṛ,चकवंड़ ćakwaṅṛ, s.m. The plant Cassia tora (esteemed a remedy for ringworm); — a common weed bearing a long legume (used as a pot-herb).

s.m.(f.?) = H چکوندا चकौंदा ćakauṅdā, चकूंदा ćakūṅdā, s.m. = H چکونڐ चकवंड ćakwaṇḍ, चकौंड ćakauṅḍ, s.m. The plant Cassia tora (esteemed a remedy for ringworm); — a common weed bearing a long legume (used as a pot-herb).
more: s. m. A species of cassia (Cassia tora) esteemed a remedy for ringworms.

def: s.m. (prov.) = H چکوته चुकौता ćukauta, s.m. = H چکوتي चुकौती ćukautī, s.f. = ćukautā, q.v.
s.m. The ruddy goose (commonly called the Brāhmanī duck; syn. ćakwā; surḵẖāb); — a lark.
more: s. m. 1. A lark. 2. An aquatick bird, said to be the same as the سرخاب surḵẖāb, q. v.

def: s.m. Ringbone (in a horse's foot; — syn. Jakrā wal).
more: s. m. The name of a disease in horses.

def: s.f. A kind of ringworm.
adj. (f. -ī), Glittering, shining brilliantly, resplendent, dazzling.
s.f. A kind of musical instrument made of wood (used by Mǒg̠als).
more: s. f. A kind of musical instrument made of wood, used by Mughals.

def: s.f. A kind of dagger (worn on the waist); a small knife (cf. ćāk, ćāqū, ćakkū).
more: n. f. 1. A dagger or knife. 2. A kind of musical instrument made of wood.
1. s. f. A kind of dagger worn on the waist. 2. adj. f. Resplendent.

def: s.f. = ćaqćaqī, q.v. s.f. A kind of musical instrument made of wood (used by Mǒg̠als).

s.f. = ćakācauṅdh, q.v. s.f. The state of being in a whirl, or dazed, or dazzled; the effect on the eyes of a sudden flash of light; — ćakāćauṅdh lagnā, ćakāćauṅdh-meṅ ānā (-se), To be dazzled or dazed (by); to be confounded or bewildered
adj. (f. -ī), Dazzied (by light); weak-eyed, dim-sighted, purblind.

ety: see ćakāćauṅdh, of which it is a var. form
def: s.f. The musk rat, Sorex cœrulescens, or S. cœrulens (syn. ćhućhūṅdarī).

ety: see ćakāćauṅdh, of which it is a var. form
def: s.f. The musk rat, Sorex cœrulescens, or S. cœrulens (syn. ćhućhūṅdarī).
s.m. A kind of swing, a merry-go-round.
